codeReview.summary: The fix improves determinism versus the prior flaky assertion, but it still does not guarantee failure on full rewrites. The new preserved-contentHash check can pass even when all chunk IDs are rewritten, so the minimal-write correctness signal remains insufficient for the stated contract.

issues.description: The new invariant intersects initial and final chunk `contentHash` values (`preservedHeadHashes.count > 0`). `contentHash` is content-based and intentionally stable across re-projections (`ProjectionIdentity.chunkContentHash` in `AgentLens/Services/ProjectionPipelineService.swift`), so a full rewrite that regenerates all chunk IDs can still preserve overlapping content hashes and pass this assertion. This does not satisfy the required 'fails on full rewrites' guarantee.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript skeleton does not show the required reads of `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, although the skill procedure mandates both in step 1.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill contract in `.factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md` step 1 requires reading both files; transcript skeleton for session `33476199-e724-4750-a10a-3f67b1e55dcc` contains mission/AGENTS reads but no references to those library files.

summary: Reviewed the fix feature handoff, transcript skeleton, skill contract, prior failed review, and both commit diffs (`9f97571` and `fda3888`). The fix addresses flakiness but still fails the original correctness requirement because the new content-hash overlap assertion can pass under full rewrites.
